*�����������������
The webRTCPanelRcv COMP is a component that can be used to receive a TouchDesigner COMP's panel over a WebRTC connection with full panel interactivity support when shared from a webRTCPanel.
It requires a signalingClient COMP connected to a supported signaling server.

2
*����������������� ># Text Component callbacks

def onValueChange(comp, value, prevValue):
	"""
	Called whenever the text component's value parameter changes for any reason including typing
	in the text window or changing the parameter directly. The callback is equivalent to the
	onValueChange in the parameter exectute DAT.
	
	Args:
		comp: the text component
		value: the new value
		prevValue: the previous value
	"""
	#debug('\nonValueChange comp:', comp.path, '- new value: ', value, ', prev value: ', prevValue )
	return
	
def onFocus(comp):
	"""
	Called when one of the text components viewers gets keyboard focus. 
	
	Args:
		comp: the text component
	"""
	#debug('\nonFocus comp:', comp.path)
	return

def onFocusEnd(comp, info):
	"""
	Called when one of the text component's viewers loses keyboard focus. In regular editing mode
	this is right after the contents are saved back to the value parameter. This function is called
	everytime the viewer loses focus, even if the text does not change.
	
	Args:
		comp: the text component
		info: a dictionary containing information about the event including:
			reason: a string indicating what caused the viewer to lose focus e.g. 'enter', 'escape', 'tab', 'unknown'
	
	"""
	#debug('\nonFocusEnd comp:', comp.path, '- info:\n', info)
	return
	
def onTextEdit(comp):
	"""
	Called each time the contents of the text component viewer change i.e. when typing a character,
	deleting text, cutting or pasting. 
	
	Args:
		comp: the text component
	"""
	#debug('\nonTextEdit comp:', comp.path, '- text: ', comp.editText)
	return
	
def onTextEditEnd(comp, value, prevValue):
	"""
	Called when the user finishes editing and the value has been changed. Not called in 'Continuous Edit' mode. Use
	onTextEdit and onFocusEnd when using continuous editing.

    Note: Any Python undo blocks created by this callback will be included in the undo step for the change to the text field.
	
	Args:
		comp: the text component
		value: the new value
		prevValue: the previous value
	"""
	#debug('\nonTextEditEnd comp:', comp.path, '- new value: ', value, ', prev value: ', prevValue )
	return



2
*�����������������+# me - this DAT
# logger - the logger COMP to which this callback DAT is attached

def onMessageLogged(info):
	"""
	onMessageLogged() is called once everytime a message is logged.

	info - A dict with the following keys
		logItemDict - the referenced COMP of the parameter Comptoexport

	"""
	return

*�����������������s# me - this DAT.
# 
# dat - the changed DAT
# rows - a list of row indices
# cols - a list of column indices
# cells - the list of cells that have changed content
# prev - the list of previous string contents of the changed cells
# 
# Make sure the corresponding toggle is enabled in the DAT Execute DAT.
# 
# If rows or columns are deleted, sizeChange will be called instead of row/col/cellChange.


def onTableChange(dat):
	ext.WebRTCPanelRcvExt.SendMouseData(dat)
	return

def onRowChange(dat, rows):
	return

def onColChange(dat, cols):
	return

def onCellChange(dat, cells, prev):
	return

def onSizeChange(dat):
	return

2
*�����������������vC"""
Extension classes enhance TouchDesigner components with python. An
extension is accessed via ext.ExtensionClassName from any operator
within the extended component. If the extension is promoted via its
Promote Extension parameter, all its attributes with capitalized names
can be accessed externally, e.g. op('yourComp').PromotedFunction().

Help: search "Extensions" in wiki
"""
import json
import uuid
from typing import List, Optional
import numpy as np
from numpy import array
from packaging import version

import TDFunctions as TDF
from TDStoreTools import StorageManager

class WebRTCPanelRcvExt:	
	"""
	The WebRTC Remote Panel Receiver COMP is a component that can be used to receive
	a TouchDesigner COMP's panel over a WebRTC connection from a matching WebRTC
	Remote Panel..

	It requires a signalingClient COMP connected to a supported signalingServer
	COMP.

	Before getting started with WebRTC, it is highly recommended to give a read to
	the introduction available on MDN Web Docs.

	To get a better understanding of what is WebRTC in TouchDesigner, it is
	recommended to have a look at the WebRTC page and the WebRTC COMP page.

	Follow the setup guidelines from the page WebRTC Remote Panel for a quick start.

	This component can be replaced by a web page designer to receive a WebRTC stream
	and a signaling client supporting TouchDesigner' Signaling API and
	signalingServer COMP.

	The WebRTC API is detailed here:
	https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/WebRTC_API

	The MediaStream API is detailed here:
	https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Media_Streams_API
	
	Visit https://docs.derivative.ca/Palette:webRTCPanelRcv for more details
	about the webRTCPanelRcv COMP and its extension.
	"""
	class Track:
		def __init__(self, **kwargs) -> None:
			self.id = kwargs.get('id', str(uuid.uuid4()))
			self.label = kwargs.get('label', '')
			self.direction = kwargs.get('direction', None)
			self.type = kwargs.get('type', None)
			self.source = kwargs.get('source', None)
			self.family = kwargs.get('family', '')
			pass

	class Connection:
		def __init__(self, **kwargs) -> None:
			self.id = kwargs.get('id', None)
			self.sender = kwargs.get('sender', None)
			self.target = kwargs.get('target', None)
			
			# Negotiation specific
			self.polite = kwargs.get('polite', False)	
			self.makingOffer = kwargs.get('makingOffer', False)
			self.ignoreOffer = kwargs.get('ignoreOffer', False) 
			self.answering = kwargs.get('answering', False) 
			self.isSettingRemoteAnswerPending = kwargs.get('isSettingRemoteAnswerPending ', False)
			
			self.tracks = []

			tableTargetId = self.target.id.val if self.target and self.target.id.val else None
			pass

	"""
	WebRTCPanelRcvExt description
	"""
	def __init__(self, ownerComp):
		# The component to which this extension is attached
		self.ownerComp = ownerComp
		self.Logger = op('logger')

		"""
		A reference to the signalingClient COMP the webRTC COMP is relying on.
		"""
		TDF.createProperty(
			self,
			'SignalingClient',
			value=self.ownerComp.par.Signalingclient.eval(),
			dependable=True,
			readOnly=False
		)

		self.signalingCheckTimer = op('timer1')

		self.messagesMetadata = {
			'apiVersion': self.ownerComp.par.Signalingapiversion.eval(),
			'compVersion': self.ownerComp.par.Version.eval(),
			'compOrigin': self.ownerComp.path,
			'projectName': project.name,
		}

		"""
		A reference to the WebRTC DAT that is at the core of the webRTC COMP.
		"""
		self.WebRTCDAT = op('webrtc1')
		self.WebRTCDAT.par.reset.pulse()

		"""
		A Connection object that is currently in the same WebRTC
		session as the webRTC COMP.
		"""
		TDF.createProperty(
			self,
			'CurrentConnection',
			value=None,
			dependable=True,
			readOnly=False
		)

		"""
		A list of message types used for WebRTC specific signaling. Those types
		are passed when the webRTC COMP is subscribing to the signalingClient
		COMP.
		"""
		TDF.createProperty(
			self,
			'SignalingClientMessagesTypes',
			value=['Offer', 'Answer', 'Ice'],
			dependable=True,
			readOnly=False
		)

		self.Subscribe()
		self.signalingCheckTimer.par.start.pulse()

	def onConnectPulse(self):		
		if self.CurrentConnection and self.WebRTCDAT[self.CurrentConnection.id, 'connection_state'].val == 'connected':
			self.Logger.Error(f'You cannot call a client you are already on a call with. Aborting.')
			return

		elif self.CurrentConnection:
			self.Logger.Error(f'An ongoing connection, likely being negotiated, was found with this client. Aborting.')
			return

		connectionId = self.WebRTCDAT.openConnection()
		sender = self.SignalingClient.AsClient
		target = None
		
		if len(self.SignalingClient.Clients):
			target = self.SignalingClient.Clients[0]
		else:
			self.Logger.Error(f'No client is available in the current signaling session.')

		connection = self.Connection(
			id=connectionId,
			sender=sender,
			target=target,
			polite=sender.properties['timeJoined'] < target.properties['timeJoined']
		)

		self.setDefaultTracks(connection)
		self.setDefaultDataChannels(connection)

		self.CurrentConnection = connection
		return

	def onActiveChange(self, par):
		self.Reset()

	def onNegotiationNeeded(self, connectionId: str) -> None:
		"""
		An event callback triggered by the WebRTC DAT when an RTCPeerConnection
		require renegotiation following changes over the peer-to-peer
		connection.  It can also occurs after opening a connection if no tracks
		were added to this connection yet.  See MDN for details.

		Args:
			connectionId (str): The ID of the RTCPeerConnection that needs
			renegotiation.
		"""		
		if not self.CurrentConnection or connectionId != self.CurrentConnection.id:
			self.Logger.Error(f'An issue occured. The connectionId did not match the CurrentConnection ID or CurrentConnection does not exist. Aborting.')
			return

		signalingState = self.WebRTCDAT.getSignalingState(connectionId)
		
		if signalingState == 'have-remote-offer' or self.CurrentConnection.answering:
			self.Logger.Warning('Already have a remote offer or answering, exiting.')
			return

		if self.CurrentConnection.makingOffer:
			self.Logger.Warning('Already making an offer, exiting.')
			return
		
		self.CurrentConnection.makingOffer = True
		self.WebRTCDAT.createOffer(connectionId)
		return

	"""
		Signaling WebRTC Messages Specifics, Received
	"""
	def OnMessageReceived(self, message: dict) -> None:
		"""
		An arbitrary message was passed from the SignalingClient to the WebRTC
		COMP.

		This message was passed because the WebRTC COMP is likely subscribed to
		a signalingClient COMP. This method is Promoted and called from the
		SignalingClient, it should not be used directly by advanced users.

		Users should instead add custom signaling message types and
		subscribe to the signalingClient COMP with a new type array. Then, new
		custom methods can be created and prefixed by onMessageReceived followed
		by the signaling message type.

		Args:
			message (dict): A dictionary following the Signaling messaging API
			and complying to the JSON Schema.
		"""

		# The following JSON keys are informations about the Signaling protocol
		# and are mandatory
		metadata = message.get('metadata', None)

		if not metadata:
			self.Logger.Error(
				f'The metadata could not be found. Invalid signaling format message. Aborting')
			return

		apiVersion = metadata.get('apiVersion', None)
		compVersion = metadata.get('compVersion', None)
		compOrigin = metadata.get('compOrigin', None)
		projectName = metadata.get('projectName', None)

		sender = message.get('sender', None)

		if not self.isVersionMatching(apiVersion):
			return
		else:
			self.Logger.Info(f'Message received from {sender}:{projectName}:{compOrigin}')

		signalingType = message.get('signalingType', '')

		if hasattr(self, 'onMessageReceived' + signalingType):
			getattr(self, 'onMessageReceived' + signalingType)(message)

		else:
			self.Logger.Warning(f'Signaling type {signalingType} appears to be unknown or no method onMessageReceived{signalingType} was associated with it.')

	def onMessageReceivedOffer(self, message: dict) -> None:
		"""
		An Offer was passed from the signaling client to the WebRTC COMP This is
		happening when the signaling client received an offer from another
		client.

		Args:
			message (dict): The formatted Signaling message received with an
			Offer.
		"""
		if not self.CurrentConnection:
			connectionId = self.WebRTCDAT.openConnection()			
						
			sender = self.SignalingClient.AsClient
			target = self.SignalingClient.GetClientByAddress(message['sender'])
			
			self.CurrentConnection = self.Connection(
				id=connectionId,
				sender=sender,
				target=target,
				polite=sender.properties['timeJoined'] < target.properties['timeJoined']
			)

			self.setDefaultTracks(self.CurrentConnection)
			self.setDefaultDataChannels(self.CurrentConnection)

		else:
			# Connection already exist, check states	
			connectionId = self.CurrentConnection.id
			self.WebRTCDAT.closeConnection(connectionId)
			self.CurrentConnection = None
			self.onMessageReceivedOffer(message)
			return

		signalingState = self.WebRTCDAT.getSignalingState(connectionId)
		
		readyForOffer = not self.CurrentConnection.makingOffer and (
			signalingState == 'stable' or self.CurrentConnection.isSettingRemoteAnswerPending
			)

		offerCollision = not readyForOffer

		ignoreOffer = not self.CurrentConnection.polite and offerCollision

		if ignoreOffer:
			self.Logger.Warning(f'Potential collision found. Ignoring offer to avoid collision.')
			return	

		self.CurrentConnection.answering = True	

		self.WebRTCDAT.setRemoteDescription(
			self.CurrentConnection.id,
			'offer',
			message['content']['sdp']
		)

		self.WebRTCDAT.createAnswer(self.CurrentConnection.id)
		return

	def onMessageReceivedAnswer(self, message: dict) -> None:
		"""
		An Answer was passed from the signaling client to the WebRTC COMP This
		is likely happening when the WebRTC COMP previously passed an Offer to a
		client.

		Args:
			message (dict): The formatted Signaling message received with an
			Answer.
		"""
		connection = self.CurrentConnection

		if connection:
			connection.isSettingRemoteAnswerPending = True
			
			self.WebRTCDAT.setRemoteDescription(
				connection.id,
				'answer',
				message['content']['sdp']
			)

			connection.isSettingRemoteAnswerPending = False

		else:
			self.Logger.Error(f'Failed to find a valid Connection, remote description wasn\'t set.')

		return

	def onMessageReceivedIce(self, message: dict) -> None:
		"""
		A new ICE was passed from the signaling client to the WebRTC COMP This
		is likely happening when the WebRTC COMP is establishing a connection
		with another WebRTC COMP, or when a connection between two peers is
		already established.

		Args:
			message (dict): The formatted Signaling message received with an ICE
			Candidate.
		"""
		connection = self.CurrentConnection

		if connection:
			self.WebRTCDAT.addIceCandidate(
				connection.id,
				message['content']['sdpCandidate'],
				message['content']['sdpMLineIndex'],
				message['content']['sdpMid']
			)

		else:
			self.Logger.Error(f'Failed to find a valid Connection, ICE candidate wasn\'t set.')

		return

	"""
		Signaling WebRTC Messages Specifics, Sending
	"""
	def onMessageSendingOffer(self, connectionId: str, localSdp: str) -> None:
		"""
		An event callback triggered when a local offer was made and a local SDP
		description was set.

		Args:
			connectionId (str): The ID of the RTCPeerConnection for which
			Signaling messages are being exchanged.
			localSdp (str): The SDP of this end of the RTCPeerConnection.
		"""
		connection = self.CurrentConnection
		message = {
			'metadata': self.messagesMetadata,
			'signalingType': 'Offer',
			'sender': self.SignalingClient.AsClient.address.val,
			'target': connection.target.address.val,
			'content': {
					'sdp': localSdp
			}
		}

		self.SignalingClient.Send(message)
		connection.makingOffer = False
		return

	def onMessageSendingAnswer(self, connectionId: str, localSdp: str) -> None:
		"""
		An event callback triggered when a local answer was made (following an
		offer being received) and a local SDP description was set.

		Args:
			connectionId (str): The ID of the RTCPeerConnection for which
			Signaling messages are being exchanged.
			localSdp (str): The SDP of this end of the RTCPeerConnection.
		"""
		connection = self.CurrentConnection
		
		message = {
			'metadata': self.messagesMetadata,
			'signalingType': 'Answer',
			'sender': self.SignalingClient.AsClient.address.val,
			'target': connection.target.address.val,
			'content': {
					'sdp': localSdp
			}
		}

		self.SignalingClient.Send(message)
		self.CurrentConnection.answering = False
		return

	def onMessageSendingIce(self, connectionId: str, sdpCandidate: str, sdpMLineIndex: int, sdpMid: str) -> None:
		"""
		An event callback triggered when a new ICE was locally made available
		and should be sent to the remote peer.

		Args:
			connectionId (str): The ID of the RTCPeerConnection for which
			Signaling messages are being exchanged.
			sdpCandidate (str): The ICE Candidate.
			sdpMLineIndex (int): The SDP MLine Index. 
			sdpMid (str): The SDP M ID.
		"""
		connection = self.CurrentConnection

		if connection:
			message = {
				'metadata': self.messagesMetadata,
				'signalingType': 'Ice',
				'sender': self.SignalingClient.AsClient.address.val,
				'target': connection.target.address.val,
				'content': {
						'sdpCandidate': sdpCandidate,
						'sdpMLineIndex': sdpMLineIndex,
						'sdpMid': sdpMid
				}
			}

			self.SignalingClient.Send(message)

		else:
			self.Logger.Error(
				f'Failed to find a valid open Connection, ICE candidate wasn\'t sent.')

		return

	"""
		Other event handlers
	"""
	def onTrack(self, connectionId: str, trackId: str, type: str) -> None:
		"""
		An event callback triggered when the remote peer created a track.

		Args:
			connectionId (str): The ID of the RTCPeerConnection on which the
			RTCDataChannel was created.
			trackId (str): The ID of the new RTC Track.
			type (str): The type of track that was opened. Video
			or Audio.
		"""
		connection = self.CurrentConnection
		self.AddTrack(connection, trackId, 'in', type)
		return
	
	def onRemoveTrack(self, connectionId: str, trackId: str, type: str) -> None:
		"""
		An event callback triggered when the remote peer removed a track.

		Args:
			connectionId (str): The ID of the RTCPeerConnection on which the
			track was removed.
			trackId (str): The ID of the track that was removed.
			type (str): The type of the track that was removed.
		"""
		connection = self.CurrentConnection
		self.RemoveTrack(connection, trackLabel=trackId)
		return

	def onDataChannel(self, connectionId: str, channelName: str) -> None:
		"""
		An event callback triggered when the remote peer created a DataChannel.

		Args:
			connectionId (str): The ID of the RTCPeerConnection on which the
			RTCDataChannel was created.
			channelName (str): The name of the RTCDataChannel that was created.
		"""
		connection = self.CurrentConnection
		if channelName in [track.label for track in connection.tracks]:
			self.Logger.Info(
				f'Silencing DataChannel Add. This end of the connection likely already called DataChannel Add and initiated the DataChannel Open event.')
			return

		track = self.Track(
			label=channelName,
			direction='in',
			type='dataChannel'
		)
		connection.tracks.append(track)

		track = self.Track(
			label=channelName,
			direction='out',
			type='dataChannel'
		)
		connection.tracks.append(track)

		return

	def onDataChannelOpen(self, connectionId: str, channelName: str) -> None:
		"""
		An event callback triggered when the state of the RTCDataChannel is
		fully opened.  Unless the DataChannel is fully opened and ready for use,
		it can be unsafe to send data over this RTCDataChannel and data could be
		lost along the way.

		Args:
			connectionId (str): The ID of the RTCPeerConnection on which the
			RTCDataChannel was opened.
			channelName (str): The name of the RTCDataChannel that was opened.
		"""
		self.Logger.Info(f'RTCDataChannel {channelName} on connection {connectionId} is ready.')
		return

	def onDataChannelClose(self, connectionId: str, channelName: str) -> None:
		"""
		An event callback triggered when the remote peer closed a DataChannel.

		Args:
			connectionId (str): The ID of the RTCPeerConnection on which the
			RTCDataChannel was closed.
			channelName (str): The name of the RTCDataChannel that was closed.
		"""
		connection = self.CurrentConnection
		self.RemoveDataChannel(connection, dataChannelLabel=channelName)

	def onConnectionStateChangeClosed(self, connectionId: str) -> None:
		"""
		An event callback triggered when one end of the RTCPeerConnection closed
		the connection or that another event caused the RTCPeerConnection to
		drop.

		This callback cleans any data related to the RTCPeerConnection that is
		still in the WebRTC COMP.

		Args:
			connectionId (str): The ID of the Connection to clean.
		"""
		self.Logger.Info(f'{connectionId} connection state changed to closed.')
		return

	"""
		RTCDataChannels send / receive specifics
	"""
	def onDataReceived(self, connectionId: str, channelName: str, data: bytearray):
		"""
		An event callback triggered when data is received over an opened
		RTCDataChannel.

		Args:
			connectionId (str): The ID of the RTCPeerConnection on which the
			RTCDataChannel data is transiting.
			channelName (str): The name of the RTCDataChannel on this end 
			of the RTCPeerConnection. 
			data (bytearray): The data as text received over this RTCDataChannel.
		"""
		if hasattr(self, 'onDataReceived' + channelName):
			getattr(self, 'onDataReceived' + channelName)(connectionId, data)

		else:
			self.Logger.Warning(f'Type {0} appears to be unknown or no method OnData{channelName} was associated with it.')

		return

	"""
	Tracks Helpers
	"""
	def GetTrackById(self, connection: Connection, trackId: str) -> Optional[Track]:
		"""
		Return a track on the specified Connection object that matches the
		specified track ID.

		Args:
			connection (Connection): A Connection object that holds the Track to
			look for.
			trackId (str): The ID of the track to look for.

		Returns:
			Optional[Track]: The track object to return. None if no match was
			found.
		"""
		for track in connection.tracks:
			if track.id == trackId:
				return track

		return None

	def GetTrackByLabel(self, connection: Connection, trackLabel: str) -> Optional[Track]:
		"""
		Return a track on the specified Connection object that matches the
		specified track Label.

		Args:
			connection (Connection): A Connection object that holds the Track to
			look for.
			trackLabel (str): The Label of the track to look for.

		Returns:
			Optional[Track]: The track object to return. None if no match
			was found.
		"""
		for track in connection.tracks:
			if track.label == trackLabel:
				return track

		return None

	"""
	DataChannels Helpers
	"""
	def GetDataChannelById(self, connection: Connection, dataChannelId: str) -> Optional[Track]:
		"""
		Return a DataChannel on the specified Connection object that matches the
		specified dataChannel ID.

		Args:
			connection (Connection): A Connection object that holds the
			dataChannel to look for.
			dataChannelId (str): The ID of the dataChannel to return.

		Returns:
			Optional[Track]: The track object to return. None if no match
			was found.
		"""
		for track in connection.tracks:
			if track.id == dataChannelId:
				return track

		return None

	def GetDataChannelsByLabel(self, connection: Connection, dataChannelLabel: str) -> List[Track]:
		"""
		Return a list of DataChannels (Track objects) on the specified
		Connection object that match the specified dataChannel Label.

		Args:
			connection (Connection): A Connection object that holds the
			dataChannel to look for.
			dataChannelLabel (str): The Label of the dataChannel(s) to return.

		Returns:
			List[Track]: A list of DataChannels that are available on the
			specified Connection and match the specified Label. 
			Return an empty list when no match is found.
		"""
		dataChannels = []
		for track in connection.tracks:
			if track.label == dataChannelLabel:
				dataChannels.append(track)

		return dataChannels

	"""
	Tracks specifics
	"""
	def setDefaultTracks(self, connection: Connection) -> None:
		"""
		Set some default tracks to initialize an RTCPeerConnection with on the
		current peer.

		It attempts to create some default tracks based on the Default Tracks
		Behaviour parameter on the WebRTC COMP.  This parameter only affects
		this end of the connection.

		Args:
			connection (Connection): A Connection object on which to add the
			tracks
		"""
		# Generate Video Tracks
		self.AddTrack(connection, 'TDPanel', 'in', 'video')

	def AddTrack(self, connection: Connection, trackLabel: str, direction: str, trackType: str, source=None) -> None:
		"""
		Main method to add a track.

		In the WebRTC COMP context, it is called when setting up default tracks
		while initializing the RTCPeerConnection. It is also called following a
		click event to add a track from the Track Manager.

		Args:
			connection (Connection): A Connection object on which to add the 
			track. 
			trackLabel (str): The Label of the track with which the
			track can be identified over the RTCPeerConnection.
			direction (str): Tracks being unidirectional, this argument 
			is used to identify whether the track is incoming and is added 
			following an onAddTrack event, or whether it is outgoing and was 
			added by this end of the RTCPeerConnection.
			trackType (str): Whether the track is an audio or video track.
			source (OP, optional): Mandatory when the track is outgoing.
			This can be a CHOP for an audio track or a TOP for a video
			track. Defaults to None.
		"""
		if direction == None:
			self.Logger.Error(
				f'A track direction is required. Aborting.')
			return

		elif direction == 'out':
			success = self.WebRTCDAT.addTrack(connection.id, trackLabel, trackType)

		track = self.Track(
			label=trackLabel,
			direction=direction,
			type=trackType,
			source=source
		)
		connection.tracks.append(track)

	def RemoveTrack(self, connection: Connection, trackId: str = None, trackLabel: str = None) -> None:
		"""
		Main method to remove a track. Can be called externally. While both
		trackId and trackLabel are optional arguments, one or the other must be
		filled and match a track object available in the passed Connection
		object.

		Args:
			connection (Connection):  A Connection object on which 
			to remove the track 
			trackId (str, optional): The ID of the track to be removed. 
			Defaults to None.
			trackLabel (str, optional): The Label of the track to be removed. 
			Defaults to None.
		"""

		if not trackId and not trackLabel:
			parent.Logger.Error(f'{trackId} {trackLabel}')

		track = None
		if trackId:
			track = self.GetTrackById(connection, trackId)
		elif trackLabel:
			track = self.GetTrackByLabel(connection, trackLabel)
		else:
			self.Logger.Error(
				f'Could not find a track without an ID or a Label. Aborting.')
			return

		success = self.WebRTCDAT.removeTrack(connection.id, track.label, track.type)

		connection.tracks.remove(track)
		return

	def AddDataChannel(self, connection: Connection, dataChannelLabel: str, source = None) -> None:
		"""
		Called to add a DataChannel

		Args:
			connection (Connection): A Connection object on which to add the
			DataChannel dataChannel 
			Label (str): A label to identify the
			DataChannel over the RTCPeerConnection 
			source (op, optional): An operator compatible with DataChannels. 
			Must be a DAT or a CHOP. Only DATs are fully supported. Defaults 
			to None.
		"""
		success = self.WebRTCDAT.createDataChannel(connection.id, dataChannelLabel)

		outTrack = self.Track(
			label=dataChannelLabel,
			direction='out',
			type='dataChannel',
			source=source,
			family=source.family
		)
		connection.tracks.append(outTrack)

		source = outTrack.source if outTrack.source is not None else ''

		inTrack = self.Track(
			label=dataChannelLabel,
			direction='in',
			type='dataChannel',
			source=source,
			family=source.family
		)
		connection.tracks.append(inTrack)
		
		source = inTrack.source if inTrack.source is not None else ''

	def RemoveDataChannel(self, connection: Connection, dataChannelId: str = None, dataChannelLabel: str = None) -> None:
		"""
		Remove a DataChannel from the specified Connection object and close it
		on the RTCPeerConnection.

		Args:
		connection (Connection): The specified Connection object that owns
		the DataChannels 
		dataChannelId (str, optional): A DataChannel ID
		that matches a DataChannel in the Connection object. Defaults to None.
		dataChannelLabel (str, optional): A DataChannel Label that matches a
		DataChannel in the Connection object. Defaults to None.
		"""
		dataChannels = None
		if dataChannelId:
			dataChannels = [self.GetDataChannelById(connection, dataChannelId)]
		elif dataChannelLabel:
			dataChannels = self.GetDataChannelsByLabel(
				connection, dataChannelLabel)
		else:
			self.Logger.Error(
				f'Could not find a track without an ID or a Label. Aborting.')
			return

		for dataChannel in dataChannels:
			self.WebRTCDAT.closeDataChannel(connection.id, dataChannel.label)
			connection.tracks.remove(dataChannel)

		return

	def setDefaultDataChannels(self, connection: Connection) -> None:
		"""
		Set DataChannels on the specified Connection object and initialize them
		over the RTCPeerConnection if needed

		Args:
				connection (Connection): The specified Connection object that
				owns the DataChannels
		"""

		# Generate DataChannels
		self.AddDataChannel(connection, 'MouseData', source=self.ownerComp.op('finalPanelData'))
		self.AddDataChannel(connection, 'KeyboardData', source=self.ownerComp.op('finalKeyboardData'))
	
	"""
	Par Callbacks
	"""
	def onSignalingclientChange(self, par) -> None:
		"""
		Called when the SignalingClient Par changed to trigger the Subscribe
		process again.

		Args:
			par (par): The SignalingClient Par.
		"""
		self.SignalingClient = par.eval()
		self.Subscribe()
	
	"""
	Utilities
	"""
	def Reset(self) -> None:
		"""
		Reset the WebRTC session and all states of the WebRTC COMP and its
		WebRTCExt.
		"""
		self.CurrentConnection = None
		self.WebRTCDAT.par.reset.pulse()
		self.Subscribe()
		self.signalingCheckTimer.par.start.pulse()

	def isVersionMatching(self, apiVersion: str) -> bool:
		"""
		This method is used to confirm that the Signaling API version is
		matching between clients and server.

		Args:
			apiVersion (str): A version number as a string, to compare with the
			signalingServer COMP own version.

		Returns:
			bool: A boolean that represents whether the version is matching or
			not.
		"""
		if version.parse(apiVersion) != version.parse(self.ownerComp.par.Signalingapiversion.eval()):
			self.Logger.Error(f'API version of client {apiVersion} does not match API version of server {self.ownerComp.par.Signalingapiversion.eval()}. Aborting.')
			return False

		else:
			return True

	def Subscribe(self, signalingClient=None) -> bool:
		"""
		This method is called on init of the WebRTCExt or when the
		SignalingClient Par changed.

		It is using the Subscribe features of the SignalingClient COMP.

		Args:
			signalingClient (COMP, optional): An optional SignalingClient
			COMP. Attempt to use an already registered SignalingClient COMP
			when None was passed. Defaults to None.

		Returns:
			bool: Whether the subscription to the SignalingClient succeeded
			or not.
		"""

		signalingClient = self.SignalingClient if not signalingClient else signalingClient

		success = False
		if signalingClient and hasattr(signalingClient, 'Subscribe'):
			success = getattr(signalingClient, 'Subscribe')(
				self.ownerComp, self.SignalingClientMessagesTypes)

		return success

	def signalingCheck(self) -> bool:
		"""
		This method is called regularly to keep the link alive between the
		referenced SignalingClient and the WebRTC COMP

		Returns:
				bool: Whether the link is still up and running.
		"""
		if self.SignalingClient and self.ownerComp in [subscriber['origin'] for subscriber in self.SignalingClient.Subscribers]:
			return True

		else:
			success = self.Subscribe()

			if not success:
				self.Logger.Error(
					f'Fatal error, the WebRTC COMP could not subscribe to the signaling client or no signaling client was found. Aborting.')
				return success

			return success

	def SendMouseData(self, dat):
		jsonDict = {}
		jsonDict['insideu'] = eval(dat['insideu', 1].val)
		jsonDict['insidev'] = eval(dat['insidev', 1].val)
		jsonDict['lselect'] = eval(dat['lselect', 1].val)
		jsonDict['mselect'] = eval(dat['mselect', 1].val)
		jsonDict['rselect'] = eval(dat['rselect', 1].val)

		if self.CurrentConnection:
			self.SendData(
				self.CurrentConnection.id,
				'MouseData',
				jsonDict
			)
		
		return

	def SendKeyboardData(self, **kwargs):
		self.Logger.Debug(kwargs)

		self.Logger.Info('SendKeyboardData')

		keyInfo = kwargs['keyInfo']

		jsonDict = {}
		jsonDict['type'] = ['keyup', 'keydown'][keyInfo.state]
		jsonDict['key'] = keyInfo.key
		jsonDict['code'] = keyInfo.webCode #webcode
		jsonDict['location'] = self.getKeyLocation(keyInfo)
		jsonDict['repeat'] = False
		jsonDict['isComposing'] = False
		jsonDict['ctrlKey'] = keyInfo.ctrl or keyInfo.cmd
		jsonDict['shiftKey'] = keyInfo.shift
		jsonDict['altKey'] = keyInfo.alt
		jsonDict['metaKey'] = keyInfo.cmd # https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/KeyboardEvent/metaKey

		if self.CurrentConnection:
			self.SendData(
				self.CurrentConnection.id,
				'KeyboardData',
				jsonDict
			)
		return

	def getKeyLocation(self, keyInfo):
		if keyInfo.ctrl:
			return 1 if keyInfo.lCtrl else 2 if keyInfo.rCtrl else 0
		elif keyInfo.alt:
			return 1 if keyInfo.lAlt else 2 if keyInfo.rAlt else 0
		elif keyInfo.shift:
			return 1 if keyInfo.lShift else 2 if keyInfo.rShift else 0
		elif keyInfo.cmd:
			return 1 if keyInfo.lCmd else 2 if keyInfo.rCmd else 0
		else:
			return 0
		
	def SendData(self, connectionId, dataChannelName, jsonDict: dict = None):
		self.WebRTCDAT.sendText(connectionId, dataChannelName, json.dumps(jsonDict))
		return

# webrtcDAT - the conext WebRTCPanelRcvExt DAT
# connectionId - uuid of the connection associated with the callback

def onOffer(webrtcDAT, connectionId, localSdp):
	webrtcDAT.setLocalDescription(connectionId, 'offer', localSdp, stereo=True)
	ext.WebRTCPanelRcvExt.onMessageSendingOffer(
		connectionId,
		localSdp
	)
	return

def onAnswer(webrtcDAT, connectionId, localSdp):
	webrtcDAT.setLocalDescription(connectionId, 'answer', localSdp, stereo=True)
	ext.WebRTCPanelRcvExt.onMessageSendingAnswer(
		connectionId,
		localSdp
	)
	return

def onNegotiationNeeded(webrtcDAT, connectionId):
	"""
		From MDN: Once the caller has created its
		RTCPeerConnection, created a media stream, 
		and added its tracks to the connection as shown in Starting a call,
		the browser will deliver a negotiationneeded event to the RTCPeerConnection to indicate 
		that it's ready to begin negotiation with the other peer.
	"""
	ext.WebRTCPanelRcvExt.onNegotiationNeeded(connectionId)
	return

def onIceCandidate(webrtcDAT, connectionId, candidate, lineIndex, sdpMid):
	ext.WebRTCPanelRcvExt.onMessageSendingIce(
		connectionId,
		candidate,
		lineIndex, 
		sdpMid
	)
	return

def onIceCandidateError(webrtcDAT, connectionId, errorText):
	return

def onTrack(webrtcDAT, connectionId, trackId, type):
	'''
	Adding a track to a connection triggers renegotiation by firing a negotiationneeded
	'''
	ext.WebRTCPanelRcvExt.Logger.Info(f'onTrack was called with args {connectionId} {trackId} {type}')
	ext.WebRTCPanelRcvExt.onTrack(connectionId, trackId, type)
	op('videostreamin1').par.webrtctrack = trackId
	return

def onRemoveTrack(webrtcDAT, connectionId, trackId, type):
	ext.WebRTCPanelRcvExt.onRemoveTrack(connectionId, trackId, type)
	return

def onDataChannel(webrtcDAT, connectionId, channelName):
	ext.WebRTCPanelRcvExt.onDataChannel(connectionId, channelName)
	return

def onDataChannelOpen(webrtcDAT, connectionId, channelName):
	ext.WebRTCPanelRcvExt.onDataChannelOpen(connectionId, channelName)
	return

def onDataChannelClose(webrtcDAT, connectionId, channelName):
	ext.WebRTCPanelRcvExt.onDataChannelClose(connectionId, channelName)
	return

def onData(webrtcDAT, connectionId, channelName, data):
	ext.WebRTCPanelRcvExt.onDataReceived(connectionId, channelName, data)
	return
	
def onConnectionStateChange(webrtcDAT, connectionId, newState):
	ext.WebRTCPanelRcvExt.Logger.Info(f'{connectionId} Connection state changed to {newState}')
	if newState == 'closed':
		ext.WebRTCPanelRcvExt.onConnectionStateChangeClosed(connectionId)

	return

def onSignalingStateChange(webrtcDAT, connectionId, newState):
	ext.WebRTCPanelRcvExt.Logger.Info(f'{connectionId} Signaling state changed to {newState}')
	return

def onIceConnectionStateChange(webrtcDAT, connectionId, newState):
	ext.WebRTCPanelRcvExt.Logger.Info(f'{connectionId} ICE connection state changed to {newState}')
	if newState == 'failed':
		webrtcDAT.restartIce(connectionId)

	return

def onIceGatheringStateChange(webrtcDAT, connectionId, newState):
	ext.WebRTCPanelRcvExt.Logger.Info(f'{connectionId} Gathering state changed to {newState}')
	return
